name: mess-hall
summary: Where the garrison eats. Long tables, benches, a serving counter or hearth, and the smell of a hundred meals soaked into the wood.

primary_palette.note: single table for a small garrison
three-row seating — large company, densely packed
fire-and-table hall — cooking happens here too
serving counter at one end, seating at the other

story_prompts: mid-meal — food still on plates, the guards left in a hurry
empty before the shift change, clean wood and stacked cups
a grim cultist refectory, eating in silence beneath a black banner
ruined mess, tables broken for firewood, one bench still upright
orc war-band hall, carved gouges in the table and bones on the floor
anti_patterns: don't place tables flush to walls on both sides — benches need room to slide back
don't use a single flickering torch; mess halls are lit to eat by
avoid ornate furniture — this is functional communal space
